objectives: Explain core concepts of database management systems
Write Sql queries to retrieve, filter, and aggregate data
Apply Sql joins to combine data across tables
learning_path.title: Foundations of database management systems and Sql

objectives: Design a logical data model for analytics use cases
Translate a logical model into a data warehouse-friendly structure
Identify modeling choices that improve data analytics performance
learning_path.title: Data Modeling for analytics and data warehouse design

objectives: Use Python to load, transform, and validate datasets
Implement reusable functions for data analysis tasks
Prepare analysis-ready outputs for downstream data analytics
learning_path.title: Python for data analysis workflows

objectives: Differentiate ETL vs ELT patterns and select an approach
Build a basic Etl/Elt Pipeline Development flow from source to target
Add validation steps to improve pipeline reliability
learning_path.title: Etl/Elt Pipeline Development for reliable data movement

objectives: Model pipeline dependencies and execution order
Implement scheduling and retries using Workflow Orchestration (Airflow/Dagster)
Monitor pipeline runs and handle failures
learning_path.title: Workflow Orchestration (Airflow/Dagster) for scheduled pipelines

objectives: Explain core concepts of Data Warehousing (Snowflake/Redshift/Bigquery)
Implement loading patterns aligned with ELT
Optimize query patterns for data analytics workloads
learning_path.title: Data Warehousing (Snowflake/Redshift/Bigquery) fundamentals

objectives: Map common data storage options to analytics needs
Design a basic cloud-based architecture for a data warehouse
Identify operational considerations for running analytics workloads in the cloud
learning_path.title: Cloud Platforms (Aws/Azure/Gcp) for data storage and analytics

objectives: Explain key concepts behind distributed execution and fault tolerance
Relate distributed systems tradeoffs to data pipeline design
Identify when distributed processing is required for data analytics
learning_path.title: Distributed Systems Fundamentals for scalable data processing

objectives: Use Apache Spark to transform large datasets
Implement batch processing patterns aligned with data warehousing
Validate outputs for downstream data analytics consumption
learning_path.title: Apache Spark for batch data processing

objectives: Explain core concepts of streaming data and event flows
Design a basic streaming pipeline using Stream Processing (Kafka/Flink)
Integrate streaming outputs into data storage for analytics
learning_path.title: Stream Processing (Kafka/Flink) for real-time data analytics

objectives: Combine Data Modeling and Etl/Elt Pipeline Development into an end-to-end flow
Load curated datasets into a data warehouse for data analytics
Validate analytics outputs using Sql
learning_path.title: End-to-end data warehouse pipeline: modeling, loading, and analytics

objectives: Apply Workflow Orchestration (Airflow/Dagster) patterns for production pipelines
Design reliability practices for batch and streaming workloads
Align data storage and data warehousing choices with data analytics requirements
learning_path.title: Operationalizing analytics: orchestration and reliability at scale

matched_occupation.description: Data engineers develop the architecture needed to process, manage, and store large amounts of data which will be used by data scientists for analysis. They design the infrastructure and maintain data pipelines and warehouses to leverage data for strategic advantage.
matched_occupation.label: data engineer
